title: Surgeries, advice and public meetings | Damian Hinds
meta_description: Damian holds regular advice surgeries in East Hampshire. There are generally two surgeries each fortnight and individual appointments are for 15 minutes.Sometimes dates have to be changed because of other commitments. Appointments are essential. To make an appointment, please call 0207 219 7057.In an emergency, if the dates are too far away, or if you are housebound, I will try to come to you, diary permitting.Before requesting a surgery appointment please do:- Check whether the issue can be sorted direct (see Resources section below),

h3: Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire
text_excerpt: Surgeries, advice and public meetings | Damian Hinds Skip to main content Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire Main navigation Home About News Campaigns In Parliament Surgeries, advice and public meetings Contact Tickets for PMQs and tours of Parliament Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire In the Community Surgeries, advice and public meetings Tweet The role of an MP Members of Parliament are elected to the House of Commons to represent the interests and concerns of all the people who live in their constituency, whether they voted for them at the General Election or not. They are only able to deal with issues raised by people who live in their constituency, called constituents. MPs consider and vote on legislation and use their position to ask government ministers questions about current issues. They split their time between working in Parliament and working in the constituency. In Parliament, MPs spend their time fighting for the interests of their constituents, attending debates, scrutinising and voting on legislation, and attending meetings. In the constituency, MPs hold advice surgeries for their constituents to come and talk to them about local issues and problems, attend meetings and community events, as well as visiting local organisations and businesses. Members of Parliament are able to help with all matters for which Parliament or central government is responsible. Click here for the full list of government departments, agencies and public bodies. When a constituent writes to their MP, the MP will then write to the relevant department or official or the Minister involved. Many problems are solved in this way. Alternatively, if a constituent is happy for the issue to be made public, an MP can ask an oral or written question, secure a debate or petition Parliament. Surgeries Damian holds regular advice surgeries in East Hampshire on Friday afternoons. Each appointment is 15 minutes long and must be booked ahead of time. To make an appointment, please email the office using the details below. You will need to provide your full name, full address with postcode and contact telephone number, as parliamentary protocol prohibits Damian from acting for constituents who are represented by another MP. Sometimes we may ask you for your date of birth to verify your identity. In line with parliamentary security guidance, you may be subject to a bag search on arrival. Please do try to leave large bags and coats at home where possible as they may not be permitted in the meeting room. You have the option of attending the surgery in person or requesting an online appointment. Before requesting a surgery appointment please do: Check whether the issue can be sorted direct (please refer to the 'when you need help' section of this page). Think about whether we could start to help you more quickly if you phoned or emailed rather than waiting for the next available surgery appointment. Please note that Members of Parliament are not permitted to intervene in any matters of a legal nature and cannot offer legal advice. Members of Parliament can usually only assist with issues that are the responsibility of the UK Parliament. Often the right person to contact will be a local councillor, Citizens Advice or an ombudsman. They are unlikely to be able to assist with private disputes. MPs do not have any jurisdiction over local Council decisions, so please contact your local Councillor (see below) if you wish to have the assistance of an elected representative in pursuing an issue with a local Council. The full list of services provided by Hampshire County Council can be found here . The full list of services provided by East Hampshire District Council can be found here . The full list of services provided by Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council can be found here . Town Council websites are also useful guides to local services: Petersfield Town Council Alton Town Council Councillors Find your District Councillor (East Hampshire District Council) Find
